incident_summary: Maintenance technician OP-3178 performed PM on BLD-221 using procedure revision 1.8 — current revision is 5.0. Incorrect torque settings applied to critical fasteners.
root_cause_detail: Maintenance procedure updated after equipment upgrade but engineering team training records not updated. Technician had not been notified of revision. Paper copy of old revision still in maintenance binder.
corrective_action: Equipment re-PM'd using current procedure. Critical fastener torque values verified with calibrated torque wrench. Equipment function tested before return to service.
preventive_action: Maintenance procedures issued via DMS only — paper binders eliminated. Technicians receive DMS notification of all procedure revisions. Revision acknowledgement mandatory before technician can perform task.
effectiveness_check: Zero outdated procedure usage by maintenance for 12 months. Monthly DMS access audit confirms all maintenance tasks use current procedure.
